title: Cutting and fitting foam for a box cushion
subtitle: Cut CMHR foam and polyester wadding to size for a gusseted sofa or chair cushion cover
excerpt: How to measure the internal dimensions of a box cushion cover, cut CMHR foam to the correct size and shape, cut and wrap polyester wadding to soften the foam edge, and fit the cushion insert into the cover for a firm and well-shaped result.
sourceNotes: City and Guilds Level 2 Upholstery syllabus.

content.text: looks full and retains its shape in use.
by 10 to 15 mm compensates for fabric tension and compression. UK regulations require CMHR (combustion-modified high-resilience) foam in all domestic upholstery.

Open the cushion cover at the zip. Measure the internal width, length, and depth (gusset height) of the cover at the widest and deepest points. Measure from seam to seam, not from edge to edge. Record the measurements and add 10 to 15 mm to the width and length for the compression allowance. The depth (gusset height) is cut to the exact internal measurement without an oversize allowance.
Step 2, mark and cut the foam

Mark the foam sheet with a marker pen against a straight-edge. For a straight cut, use a long straight-edge and score with a sharp utility knife in a single stroke, then snap the foam over a table edge. For a cleaner cut on thicker foam (75 mm / 3 in and over), use upholstery shears with long blades, cutting in a single smooth stroke. Jagged cuts compress unevenly and produce lumps in the cushion face.
Step 3, cut and wrap the polyester wadding

Cut a piece of polyester wadding polyester wadding large enough to wrap around all six faces of the foam block with 50 mm (2 in) of overlap on each edge. Wrap the foam completely, pulling the polyester wadding snug but not tight. Secure the overlapping edges with a few dabs of spray adhesive or upholstery contact adhesive. The polyester wadding layer softens the foam edge line under the cover and adds a slight resilience to the surface feel.
Step 4, fit the insert into the cover

Fold the polyester wadding-wrapped foam in half lengthwise, insert the folded end into the cover through the zip opening, and open the foam back out inside the cover while pushing it toward the far end. Ease the foam into the corners of the cover; a long, flat tool (filling knife or ruler) helps push the foam into the cover corners without tearing the polyester wadding. Close the zip. The cushion top face should be smooth and the gusset seams should sit straight without puckering.
Cutting the foam to the exact cover dimensions without a compression allowance.
The cushion will feel loose in the cover and the top face will look sunken. Cut 10 to 15 mm oversize in both directions.
Buying foam by density number alone.
The same density number in different foam grades (reflex, CMHR, memory foam) behaves completely differently. Specify CMHR foam and the appropriate firmness grade (usually medium for seating).
